title: Ever since Elon Musk took over, banning social media accounts became common: Iltija Mufti
content: She said banning the social media accounts has impacted her ability “to raise issues of public importance and reach out to people over misgovernance”

title: AICC begins consultations with Kerala leaders in New Delhi to decide on Congress’ CM candidate
content: The high command, according to a party insider, is reportedly seeking bipartisan support of top leaders, including former KPCC presidents, to restore the period of organisational and alliance stability, which delivered the Congress the momentous win

title: MGNREGS workers togo on strike on May 15
content: NREGA Sangharsh Morcha and All India Kisan Sabha have called for the strike against the government’s decision to discontinue the scheme.

title: ‘Bangla Pokkho’ founder Garga Chatterjee arrested for spreading EVM misinformation
content: Bangla Pokkho is a pro-Bengali advocacy organisation, and Mr. Chatterjee is often found campaigning for prioritising the Bengali language and Bengalis over other languages and communities in West Bengal

title: Trinamool’s land policy delayed West Bengal border fencing: MHA’s 2023 affidavit in Supreme Court
content: On December 11, 2023, the Ministry of Home Affairs informed the Supreme Court that the West Bengal government had not adopted the LARR Act 2013, which allows land acquisition for public purposes

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled highlights: CBI takes custody of man detained by Nashik Police
content: The schedule for the re-examination would be announced within the “next seven to 10 days”, according to the DG of NTA, Abhishek Singh

title: Tamil Nadu’s political transition, growth story faultlines
content: The State’s development model is under pressure from changing realities, which the new Chief Minister needs to address

title: Baltimore ship crash: U.S. slaps criminal cases against Singapore, Chennai firms and Indian national
content: They have been charged with conspiracy, wilfully failing to immediately inform U.S. Coast Guard of a known hazardous condition, obstruction of an agency proceeding, and false statements

title: A society at large under question: rebuild a culture where safety of children is collective responsibility
content: The sexual assault and murder of a minor girl in Maharashtra has once again exposed the disturbing vulnerabilities that continue to confront children in our society

title: Floor test will be smooth; have received letters from EPS, Velumani factions: T.N. Assembly Speaker
content: “It is by the grace of God that Mr. Vijay has won. Our leader is going to prove his majority on May 13,” says Prabhakar, who exudes confidence that the young first-time MLAs will exceed expectations

title: West Godavari district of A.P. bets big on green silica industry
content: The paddy-rich district aims to process husk into precipitated silica and silica gel, which have high demand in automotive, cosmetric and other industries, ensuring monetisation of farm waste

title: Quantum jump in followers of T.N. CMO’s social media handles after Vijay’s swearing-in
content: The number of followers on Instagram soared from four lakh to 2.1 million after C. Joseph Vijay took over as the Chief Minister

title: Elephant dies after fall near Nadugani ghat road
content: The elephant is suspected to have slipped from a cliff nearly 30 metres high before crashing onto the concrete structure below, say forest officials

title: Tamil Nadu politics Highlights: T.N. Assembly to telecast proceedings live for first time on May 13
content: The officials say that the live telecast includes floor test; CM Vijay meets leaders of VCK, IUML, TNCC and “rebel AIADMK leaders”

title: A nearly 40-year-old pending case spurs Supreme Court to declare speedy trial a ‘human right’
content: Bench directs a sweeping enquiry into the backlog, judicial vacancies and bail pleas languishing in trial courts across  Uttar Pradesh

title: CBI registers case to probe alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, forms special teams
content: The CBI has dispatched special teams to various locations to follow up on leads. The allegations indicate a possible compromise of the sanctity and integrity of the examination process, the agency has said.

title: Over ₹1 crore cash, AK-47 rifles recovered from Maoist hideouts, say police
content: The operations were undertaken based on information provided by some surrendered Maoists, the police say

title: Supreme Court to hear ED plea against bail granted to accused in West Bengal coal scam case
content: Additional Solicitor General S. V. Raju sought cancellation of the anticipatory bail, contending that Majee had been absconding and that the case involved the large-scale plunder of national resources

title: A woman’s assertion of independence must not be termed ‘defiance’ in marriage: Supreme Court
content: The expectation that a woman must sacrifice her career and conform to traditional notions of an obedient wife reflects a line of reasoning that is archaic, the justice said

title: Opposition parties are spreading panic over PM’s voluntary, precautionary appeal: BJP
content: Despite 70 days of war in West Asia, there has been no shortage of oil in the country, fuel prices have not increased, BJP spokesperson says

title: Behind bars by choice: Hyderabad’s Chanchalguda Jail now lets citizens ‘feel the jail’ at ₹2,000 for 24 hours
content: The 24-hour “Feel the Jail” experience has been priced at ₹2,000, while those opting for a shorter 12-hour stay will pay ₹1,000

title: Infiltrator killed by Army near the LoC in Poonch
content: An Army spokesman said soldiers detected “suspicious movement around 300 metres inside own territory in the general area of Krishna Ghati sector along the LoC at around 4 p.m.”

title: ‘Don’t want to be part of biased exercise’: Rahul Gandhi records dissent on CBI director’s selection
content: LoP Rahul Gandhi said that despite repeated written requests, he was not provided with the self-appraisal reports or 360-degree reports of the eligible candidates.

title: Former Army personnel accused of killing three people, dies in police encounter in Uttar Pradesh
content: Gurpreet Singh, the suspect, committed the first murder on May 10 while traveling between Prayagraj and Chandauli, second and third on May 11, one on a train, another in a hospital, respectively

title: Education Ministry gives fresh push for implementation of PM-SHRI in Bengal, Kerala, and T.N. post elections
content: Launched in September 2022, this five-year initiative aims to transform over 14,500 schools into “exemplar institutions” that lead the way in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020; only a limited time is left for the scheme to complete its five-year duration, the Ministry stated in a letter issued to the States

title: Chinese claims of ‘on site support’ to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor ‘corroborates’ what was known earlier: MEA
content: “Nations must reflect on whether supporting terror infrastructure affects their reputation and standing,” warns MEA; engineer confirms role of Chinese personnel in last year’s India-Pakistan clash

title: BJP MLA Tapas Roy sworn in as Pro-tem Speaker of West Bengal Assembly
content: Tapas Roy is among the seniormost MLAs elected to the 18th West Bengal Legislative Assembly; according to BJP sources, he may be nominated for the post of Speaker

title: Four cheetah cubs die at Kuno in suspected leopard attack
content: The deaths in Madhya Pradesh hold significance as the four cubs were the first to be born in the open forest since India’s reintroduction programme for the big cat species — Project Cheetah — was launched in 2022; this is the largest number of cheetah casualties at Kuno in one instance, which witnessed the birth of several cubs in recent months.

title: IMD unveils weather model to provide ‘block level’ forecast of monsoon journey
content: It has been a long-standing aim of the IMD to provide hyper local forecasts to enable farmers to time their sowing precisely

title: Minister urges Andhra nurses to earn Kerala-like recognition in Guntur
content: Addressing the International Nurses Day celebrations at Government Medical College, Satya Kumar Yadav says nurses provide emotional support and comfort to patients through compassionate service and care

title: Tamil Nadu government appoints Rickey Pandit Vettrivel as OSD (Political) to Chief Minister
content: CPI(M) state secretary P. Shanmugam said that it was unacceptable to appoint an astrologer as the Officer-on-Duty (Politics) to the Chief Minister

title: The superpower trap: Who has more cards - Trump or Xi?
content: President Trump's China visit occurs amid a US-Iran stalemate, with analysts suggesting Xi Jinping holds more leverage. Many in Beijing view Trump as a symptom of liberal democracy's instability and American decline, validating Xi's "rise of the East" worldview. This perception of US weakness could lead China to miscalculate and test Washington.

title: IPL: GT storm to top of table as Rabada leads SRH demolition in 82-run thrashing
content: Gujarat Titans secured a dominant 82-run victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad. Sai Sudharsan and Washington Sundar built a crucial partnership to set a competitive total. The Gujarat bowling attack then dismantled Hyderabad's batting order. Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder were instrumental in restricting the opposition. This win propelled Gujarat Titans to the top of the IPL 2026 standings.

title: 'Sent from my Windows Phone': Old email screenshot reveals Nadella-Altman bond since 2015
content: A recent X screenshot reveals Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's early 2015 emails with OpenAI's Sam Altman, predating ChatGPT's fame. These exchanges, sent from a Windows Phone, highlight a decade-long strategic dialogue, not a sudden partnership. This resurfaces Nadella's regret over exiting the smartphone market, a decision he called a 'biggest mistake'.

title: EAM’s bilateral with Iran’s Araghchi on Friday, energy disruption & connectivity on agenda
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar will host his Iranian counterpart for bilateral talks focusing on energy and connectivity concerns, including stranded LPG tankers and Chabahar port operations. This meeting, occurring during the BRICS foreign ministers' gathering, aims to address regional conflicts and foster cooperation among member states.

title: CBI takes over Suvendu ex-aide murder probe
content: The CBI has filed an FIR and initiated an investigation into the murder of Chandranath Rath, former personal assistant to West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari. A seven-member SIT, led by a DIG-rank officer, has been formed to probe the case, with a meeting held at CBI headquarters to review developments and seek remand of arrested suspects.

title: Monsoon may hit Andamans this week: IMD
content: The India Meteorological Department has launched an AI-enabled monsoon forecasting system offering localized weather information up to four weeks in advance for farmers. A pilot service for Uttar Pradesh provides high-resolution rainfall forecasts at 1km resolution for up to 10 days, utilizing advanced AI and observational data.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'People's will through lawmakers can bring religious reforms': Supreme Court
content: Constitutional courts should be extremely reluctant to alter religious practices, a nine-judge Supreme Court bench cautioned.  Only the will of the people, expressed through elected representatives in Parliament and assemblies, can bring about reforms in religious customs.  The court emphasized that it's impossible to scrutinize every religious practice.

title: Delayed by a year due to tech glitches, livestock census to be out after June
content: The 21st Livestock Census report's release has been postponed to after June due to technical issues in data compilation and validation. This quinquennial exercise, which concluded its data collection in February last year, aims to provide crucial updated figures on livestock and poultry populations for policy decisions.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Insurance pool of Rs 13,000 crore for Indian ships
content: The Indian government has launched a $1.5 billion Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool, backed by a $1.4 billion sovereign guarantee, to offer risk cover for Indian vessels on international routes, including war zones. This initiative aims to reduce shipping insurance premiums and guarantee coverage even when global insurers withdraw due to rising risks.

title: Supreme Court to hear ED plea to cancel bail to West Bengal coal scam 'kingpin'
content: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear the Enforcement Directorate's plea to cancel the bail of alleged coal smuggling kingpin Anup Majee. While issuing a notice, the court noted the ED's prolonged investigation. Majee, accused in a Rs 2,700 crore scam, claims cooperation, but the ED highlights his accountant's absconding status and alleged payoffs.

title: Kevin Goldschmidt faces backlash as Mike Vrabel rumors spark fresh NFL buzz
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and former NFL insider of the New York Times’ Dianna Russini, have continued to dominate headlines even as their spouses remain silent about the alleged affair drama, sparking chaos among millions. While Mike Vrabel has gone back to work, Dianna Russini’s recent moment with her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt, has sparked shockwaves across the league.

title: Months after Marshawn Kneeland’s death, girlfriend shares baby update
content: Weeks before welcoming Marshawn Kneeland's child, his girlfriend Catalina Mancera celebrated a heartwarming baby shower in Michigan.  The event, filled with love and support, honored the late Cowboys star's memory.  Catalina expressed deep gratitude for the community's unwavering kindness as she prepares to become a mother, surrounded by a network dedicated to her and the baby.

title: Jose Mourinho in talks for shock Real Madrid return after 13 years away
content: Jose Mourinho is reportedly nearing a sensational return to Real Madrid following the club’s disastrous 2025-26 season. With dressing-room tensions, poor performances and growing pressure surrounding several stars, Madrid are believed to view Mourinho as the ideal figure to restore authority and stability. The Portuguese manager is currently in talks with the club and could leave Benfica once their season officially concludes.

title: From surviving gang-ravaged streets to pinnacle of NBA basketball: The Isiah Thomas way
content: In this exclusive interview with TimesofIndia.com, Isiah Thomas reflects on his journey from Chicago’s gang-ravaged West Side to NBA greatness. The Detroit Pistons legend spoke about survival instincts, leadership, the “mental over physical” philosophy behind the Bad Boys era, business lessons after basketball, his long-held vision of positionless basketball, and more.

title: Introspect on reputational cost of aiding terror exporter Pakistan, India tells China
content: India's MEA urged China to consider the reputational damage of backing Pakistan's terrorist infrastructure, following reports of Chinese tech assistance during last year's Operation Sindoor. The ministry stated these reports corroborated existing knowledge, emphasizing that supporting such infrastructure impacts a nation's standing.

title: Farooq joins liquor debate, says even Sheikh Abdullah didn’t favour prohibition
content: National Conference president Farooq Abdullah weighed in on a liquor ban debate in Jammu and Kashmir, citing his father's 1977 stance that revenue generation was key. He argued that prohibition wouldn't stop consumption, as people would source alcohol from outside. Abdullah also questioned critics' silence when liquor shops previously opened across the region.

title: Rahul Dev recalls insensitive media behaviour at Mukul Dev’s funeral
content: Rahul Dev recently opened up about the difficult time following the death of his brother, actor Mukul Dev, and called out the insensitive behaviour of some sections of the media during the funeral proceedings. Mukul Dev passed away on May 23, 2025, following a brief illness and a period in the ICU. Rahul Dev said he was disturbed by the way personal grief was being covered during his brother’s final journey.

title: Brandon Clarke death: new reports raise questions over circumstances
content: Memphis Grizzlies player Brandon Clarke has passed away at 29. Initial reports focused on his character. However, new details reveal a complex situation. Investigations suggest a possible overdose. This follows a recent arrest for drug trafficking and a high-speed chase. Clarke had also faced significant injury setbacks. These events paint a somber picture of his final months.

title: Terrorist killed, Poonch infiltration bid foiled
content: Army troops foiled an infiltration attempt in Poonch's Krishna Ghati sector on Tuesday, neutralizing one terrorist approximately 300 meters inside the Indian side of the Line of Control. This incident follows several other thwarted infiltration bids in the Jammu region in recent months, highlighting ongoing vigilance along the LoC.

title: IPL: Shubman Gill's 'it will not be easy for them' message goes viral
content: Gujarat Titans secured a commanding 82-run victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad. This win propelled Gujarat Titans to the top of the Indian Premier League 2026 standings. Half-centuries from Sai Sudharsan and Washington Sundar set a strong total. Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder then dismantled the Sunrisers Hyderabad batting lineup. Gujarat Titans achieved their fifth consecutive win.

title: Iltija Mufti blames ‘NC-BJP nexus’ for her X suspension
content: Iltija Mufti, daughter of Mehbooba Mufti, claims the National Conference, in league with the BJP, suspended her X account. She alleges this follows her criticism of the NC and the proposed removal of Urdu as a mandatory language. Her Facebook page was also recently deactivated, impacting her political outreach.

title: 2 Pakistanis among 5 sent to 25-day custody for terror acts in J&K
content: Five people have been sent to judicial custody for 25 days. The group includes two Pakistani nationals. This development is part of an NIA case concerning terror activities. Earlier, a grenade and bullets were seized from a suspect in Srinagar. His interrogation led to the arrest of the other four individuals.

title: The insomniac-in-chief, a President who can’t log off
content: President Trump's late-night social media activity, characterized by numerous posts on his Truth Social platform, is raising concerns about his focus and stamina. Critics and even some Republicans privately express unease, comparing his behavior to an "aggrieved influencer" rather than a commander-in-chief, especially as the nation faces international and domestic challenges.

title: PM Modi wants Indians to pause gold buying: How much forex can be saved?
content: India’s gold imports hit a record $71.98 billion in 2025-26, rising over 24% from $58 billion a year earlier. This was largely due to skyrocketing global gold prices, which have bumped up the value of imported gold.

title: Sai Sudharsan equals Virat Kohli's massive IPL record
content: Sai Sudharsan's composed 61 off 44 balls guided Gujarat Titans to 168/5 against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His innings marked his third consecutive IPL season with over 500 runs, a feat previously achieved by only a few elite players. Sudharsan continues to be a standout performer with an impressive strike rate.

title: Rakesh Bedi reacts to Rs 1 crore bonus buzz after ‘Dhurandhar’ success
content: Veteran actor Rakesh Bedi has become one of the most talked-about names after the blockbuster success of Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge, thanks to his fan-favourite character Jameel Jamali and the viral dialogue, “Bachcha hai tu mera.” Amid reports claiming that the actor received a Rs 1 crore bonus from the makers following the film’s success, Bedi has now reacted to the ongoing buzz with his trademark humour.

title: ‘Removed uterus left in box’: 4 women die after C-section surgery in Kota
content: Another woman has died from post-cesarean section complications at Kota's New Medical College Hospital, marking the fourth fatality in a week. Pinki Mahawar, 30, succumbed after being transferred from JK Lon Hospital, with her husband alleging a delayed referral. The incident has led to protests and calls for accountability regarding safety protocols in government hospitals.

title: CSK likely to rope in UAE-raised Karnataka batter Noronha
content: Chennai Super Kings are reportedly set to sign Karnataka's Macneil Hadley Noronha as an injury replacement for Ramakrishna Ghosh for the remainder of IPL 2026. Noronha, who had a stellar C.K. Nayudu Trophy campaign, impressed during trials with multiple franchises. This move comes as CSK faces a season heavily disrupted by injuries to key players.

title: Jana Nayagan OTT battle, Peddi trailer, NTRNEEL glimpse: Top 5 South stories
content: The South Indian film scene is abuzz with major updates.  'Jana Nayagan' OTT rights are seeing a bidding war following Vijay's political ascent. Suriya's compassionate handling of a fan security breach went viral. 'Peddi' trailer launch is now in Mumbai, while Ravi Mohan and Kenishaa's stage chemistry sparks 'indirect proposal' talk.  Fans eagerly await the 'NTRNEEL' glimpse.

title: Yogi of northeast? How 'outsider' Himanta emerged as BJP's next-gen leader
content: After winning the BJP a brute majority in the 2026 Assam assembly polls, the question now is whether Himanta Biswa Sarma stands at par with chief ministers like Yogi Adityanath and Devendra Fadnavis, who are widely regarded as disciplined Sangh loyalists and potential next generation leaders of the BJP in the post Modi-Shah era.

title: Why Vijay's floor test in Tamil Nadu is really a survival test for AIADMK
content: Tamil Nadu's trust vote for CM-designate Vijay is overshadowed by a potential AIADMK rupture. Senior rebels are questioning party chief Palaniswami's leadership, accusing him of seeking an understanding with the rival DMK. This internal strife could signal the AIADMK's terminal decline as its MLAs consider shifting loyalty.

title: Penguins eye $7.8M Stuart Skinner trade to Senators in latest NHL shake-up
content: Stuart Skinner has been a potential free agency target for the Ottawa Senators since his days with the Edmonton Oilers, and now he’s being linked to the Pittsburgh Penguins. Ottawa could be a player because of defensive inconsistencies and poor penalty kill rankings, NHL insiders say. His $7.8 million contract situation and Pittsburgh’s goalie competition may open the door for a major offseason move.

title: Petrol & diesel prices frozen for 4 years - how deep is India's fuel dilemma?
content: India assures stable fuel supplies despite global disruptions and rising crude prices. However, unchanged domestic rates are causing significant losses for oil marketing companies, leading to a pressing question about potential price hikes. The government faces a fiscal burden from absorbing price shocks through tax cuts.

title: Reports claim Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar have parted ways
content: Rumours surrounding the marriage of Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar have intensified after reports claimed that the couple has allegedly divorced after four years of marriage. Reports further suggest that cheating may have been one of the reasons behind their reported separation.

title: LeBron James admits uncertainty over retirement after Lakers playoff exit
content: LeBron James admitted he is unsure about continuing his NBA career after the Lakers were swept by the Oklahoma City Thunder. The 41-year-old said he will discuss his future with family before making a decision. James added that staying committed to the daily basketball process will play a major role. The Lakers star is entering free agency this offseason after another strong season.

title: At Tech Trial, Sam Altman Says Elon Musk Wanted 90% Stake In OpenAI
content: OpenAI counters that Musk -- who is now also an AI competitor with his xAI -- is motivated by petty revenge, having failed to seize majority control of the commercial entity.

title: 13 Killed In Israeli Strikes In Lebanon
content: Israeli strikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, including a soldier, a child and two rescue workers, the health ministry said.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation Highlights: Vijay Meets Allies To Rally Support As Row Erupts Over Astrologer's Appointment
content: Vijay's oath ceremony, which took place on Sunday, came after four days of protracted political negotiations after his party received an unprecedented mandate that split open the binary politics that Tamil Nadu has been witnessing for over 50 years

title: Meta US Employees Organise Protest Against Mouse Tracking Tech
content: The flyers encourage staffers to sign an online petition against the move, saying "workers are legally protected when they choose to organize for the improvement of working conditions."

title: Exclusive: Satellite Images Confirm Pak May Be Shielding Iran's Military Aircraft
content: Iran sent multiple aircraft including a reconnaissance and intelligence plane to Pakistan's Nur Khan airbase soon after US President Donald Trump announced the ceasefire in April, CBS News reported

title: With Eye On Iran And Trade, Trump Leaves For China To Meet Xi Jinping
content: Donald Trump sought to downplay differences with Xi over Iran and the shadow the conflict is casting on global oil markets. The two leaders are expected to discuss trade issues, Iran war, and Taiwan.

title: "Our Appeal To Prime Minister Is...": What Khan Sir Said On NEET Paper Leak
content: Neet Paper Leak: Khan Sir has called for stronger scrutiny of the NTA, alleging deep-rooted irregularities and possible internal involvement in repeated exam paper leak incidents.

title: "Biased": Rahul Gandhi Slams Process To Select Next CBI Director
content: In his two-page dissent note, he alleged that the government has reduced the selection process to a mere formality and the leader of opposition (LoP) cannot be a rubber stamp.

title: How A Hostel Owner In Rajasthan's Sikar Helped Unravel NEET Paper Leak
content: The "guess paper" had an amazing strike rate -- 135 questions had matched originals. The hostel owner and the chemistry teacher then decided to approach the local police.

title: 'I Don't Trust Them': US Senator Turns On Pak Over Iranian Aircraft Claims
content: At a time when Islamabad had been positioning itself as a neutral channel between Washington and Tehran, a US media report suggested it had allowed Iranian military planes to park on its soil.

title: 6 Devotees Killed, 14 Injured After Temple Wall Collapses In Maharashtra
content: Six devotees were killed and 12 to 14 others injured after a wall and tin sheets on the premises of a temple collapsed following strong winds and heavy rains in Maharashtra's Sangli district on Tuesday evening, police officials said.

title: 'Shock Is Coming': Uday Kotak Backs PM, Issues Warning Over Iran War
content: Kotak said the Prime Minister's remarks reflected a larger strategic concern around India's economic resilience amid geopolitical instability and rising energy risks.

title: Trump's Battleship Will Run On Nuclear Power, Carry Futuristic Weapons
content: The plan was included in the Navy's latest 30-year shipbuilding roadmap submitted to Congress. Unlike destroyers and frigates, which usually rely on gas turbines or diesel engines, the Trump battleship will use a nuclear reactor for propulsion.

title: "Nobody Even Looked At Me": Rajinikanth Shares Profound Experience At Art Of Living
content: Superstar Rajinikanth narrated an experience in The Art of Living which he said opened his eyes to something profound

title: Oil Shock From Iran War Drives US Consumer Inflation To 3-Year High
content: The consumer price index (CPI) rose 3.8 per cent year-on-year, up from March's 3.3 per cent figure, the US Bureau of Labour Statistics (BLS) said.

title: Coaching Centres, Scanners, Shadow Server: Intricate Web Of NEET Leak
content: NEET Leak: The first digital copy of the leaked medical entrance exam's question paper was prepared in Nashik, sources said.

title: Video Shows Putin Out And About In Moscow Amid 'Bunker' Reports
content: Putin's video, which was released late on Monday, appeared to be a visual rebuttal of those accusations and of assertions.

title: What Happens If UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Quits, Or Is Challenged?
content: Some senior cabinet ministers, according to media reports, have told Starmer to announce a plan for his exit.

title: Vijay urges Union Minister to secure the release of six T.N. fisherfolk arrested by Lankan Navy

title: 'Bad days for you know who': Himanta-Suvendu bonhomie grabs attention at Assam govt swearing-in
content: Source:  @himantabiswa/X The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. title: Returning the favour? In 1971, Iran had sheltered Pakistan assets
content: the report of Pakistan giving sanctuary to Iranian military aircraft revives one of the subcontinent's great geopolitical ironies . the claims were implausible because the Nur Khan base sits "right in the heart of the city" the US administration has stopped short of publicly accusing Islamabad of wrongdoing .

title: Thalaivar, Amma, Thalapathy: Why TN doesn’t just vote for leaders, it lives (& dies) for them
content: Aastha Jha is a writer at The Times of India, covering politics. She also writes on business, global affairs, social issues, with a focus on policy, governance and their impact on everyday lives. Her work brings together reportage and analysis, tracking developments in India and abroad while examining the broader social and economic currents shaping public discourse. Read More

title: Vijay's floor test splits AIADMK. Will Thalapathy go the Yediyurappa way?
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More
